title: Add a true race-lap ranking expert
summary: This target is inherently relative within each race-lap snapshot: only a few drivers are plausible next-lap pit candidates, so querywise ranking can learn who should be above whom better than plain logloss.
rationale: This target is inherently relative within each race-lap snapshot: only a few drivers are plausible next-lap pit candidates, so querywise ranking can learn who should be above whom better than plain logloss. CatBoost and LambdaRank literature both support optimizing within-group order directly.
implementation_hint: Create query groups as `Year_Race_LapNumber` or `Year_Race_LapNumber_CompoundRegime`. Train a ranking expert with CatBoost `YetiRankPairwise`, `LambdaMart`, or `QueryCrossEntropy` using the binary label, then blend its rank-normalized OOF with the classifier OOF. Evaluate both groupwise AUC within query and global ROC AUC.
expected_effect: Most likely helps top-of-lap ordering, especially in laps with several old-tyre candidates where plain classifiers compress scores.
risk: Global ROC AUC may not improve if queries with zero or one positive dominate; ranking objectives can also be noisier on tiny groups.
